content.problemStatement: You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. Let's define the useful number of a subsequence of AAA as the number of distinct primes ppp such that ppp divides each member of the subsequence, multiplied by the length of the subsequence. Find the maximum of useful numbers of all subsequences of AAA.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-seperated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum useful number.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The subsequence [2,2,4,8][2, 2, 4, 8][2,2,4,8] has the maximum useful number. The number of distinct primes that divide each member of the subsequence is 111 (only the prime 222) and the length of the subsequence is 444, so the useful number of this subsequence is 1‚ãÖ4=41\cdot 4 = 41‚ãÖ4=4.
